title: Securing an interface element rail of a robotic surgical instrument interface
abstract: A robotic surgical instrument, comprising:a shaft;an articulation at a distal end of the shaft for articulating an end effector, the articulation being driveable by a pair of driving elements; and an instrument interface at a proximal end of the shaft, the instrument interface comprising:a chassis;an instrument interface element slideable along a guide bar for driving the pair of driving elements, wherein the pair of driving elements are fast with respect to the interface element so that a displacement of the instrument interface element with respect to the guide bar is transferred to the pair of driving elements; the chassis comprising a support element configured to interface the guide bar along at least a portion of its length; anda securing element for retaining the guide bar against the support element to thereby secure the guide bar to the chassis.
claims: Claims 1 . A robotic surgical instrument, comprising: a shaft; an articulation at a distal end of the shaft for articulating an end effector, the articulation being driveable by a pair of driving elements; and an instrument interface at a proximal end of the shaft, the instrument interface comprising: a chassis; an instrument interface element slideable along a guide bar for driving the pair of driving elements, wherein the pair of driving elements are fast with respect to the interface element so that a displacement of the instrument interface element with respect to the guide bar is transferred to the pair of driving elements; the chassis comprising a support element configured to interface the guide bar along at least a portion of its length; and a securing element for retaining the guide bar against the support element to thereby secure the guide bar to the chassis. 2. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the support element comprises a curved surface that interfaces the guide bar along at least a portion of its length. 3. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in claim 2, wherein the support element is arranged so that each surface normal to the curved surface is transverse to a longitudinal axis of the guide bar. 4. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the support element is a corner feature defining a corner, and the securing element retains the guide bar in the corner to secure the guide bar to the chassis. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in claim 4, wherein the corner feature prises two surfaces that define the corner. 6. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in claim 5, wherein the two surfaces are planar. 7. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in claim 5 or 6, wherein the two surfaces are transverse to each other. 8. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in claim 5 or 6, wherein the angle between the two surfaces is less than 180 degrees. 9. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in claim 5 or 6, wherein the angle between the two surfaces is greater than or equal to 90 degrees and less than 180 degrees. 10. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in claim 5 or 6, wherein the angle between the two surfaces is less than or equal to 90 degrees. 1 1 . A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in any of claims 5 to 10, wherein the two surfaces meet to define a join that is parallel to a longitudinal axis of the instrument shaft. 12. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the securing element comprises a shaft portion and a conical-shaped head, the shaft portion being inserted into the chassis to secure the guide bar to the chassis. 13. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in claim 12, as dependent on claim 5, wherein the shaft portion is inserted into the chassis parallel to one of the surfaces of the corner feature. 14. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in claim 12 as dependent on claim 5, or claim 13, wherein the shaft portion is inserted into the chassis so that the conical- shaped head secures the guide bar against the two surfaces of the corner feature. 15. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in any of claims 12 to 14, wherein the shaft portion is a threaded shaft portion. 16. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in any of claims 12 to 15, wherein the securing element is a countersunk screw or bolt. 17. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in any of claims 5 to 1 1 , wherein the securing element comprises a shaft and a head, the shaft being inserted into the corner feature at an angle to both surfaces of the corner feature so that a longitudinal axis of the shaft is non-parallel to both surfaces. 18. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in claim 17, wherein the shaft is inserted into the corner feature diagonally to the surfaces of the corner feature. 19. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in claim 17 or 18, wherein the securing element is a pan-head bolt. 20. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in any of claims 1 to 1 1 , wherein the securing element comprises a retaining element having a first surface shaped to engage the guide bar and being secured to the chassis to thereby secure the guide bar to the chassis. 21 . A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in claim 20 as dependent on claim 5, wherein the retaining element is a block comprising second and third surfaces angled to interface the surfaces of the corner feature. 22. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in claim 20 or 21 , wherein the securing element is secured to the chassis by one or more bolts or screws. 23. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in any of claims 1 to 1 1 , wherein the guide bar comprises a bore, and the securing element is a screw or bolt inserted into the support element through the bore to secure the guide bar to the chassis. 24. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the instrument interface element is linearly slideable along the guide bar. 25. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in claim 24, wherein the instrument interface element is linearly slideable along a longitudinal axis of the guide bar parallel to a longitudinal axis of the shaft. 26. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the instrument interface further comprises a second securing element to secure the guide bar to the chassis. 27. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in claim 26, wherein the two securing elements are located at opposing ends of the guide bar. 28. A robotic surgical instrument as claimed in claim 26 or 27, wherein the chassis comprises a second support element, and the second securing element retains the guide bar against the second support element to secure the guide bar to the chassis.
